summary: unsupported_context_too_large: context (896814 chars) exceeds shim cap (480000)
One test assertion failed in test/config/get.ts: the config command is returning a registry URL with a trailing slash when the test expects it without one.
root_cause: The test at test/config/get.ts:32 expects `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` to return 'https://my-org.registry.example.com' but it returns 'https://my-org.registry.example.com/' (with trailing slash). This indicates a change in how registry URLs are normalized or formatted by the config reading logic.
relevant_tests: test/config/get.ts: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Direct test assertion failure showing the exact mismatch: trailing slash present in actual output but not in expected value
The test explicitly asserts that config get should return the registry URL without a trailing slash
Confirms this is the only failing test in the entire suite (485 tests total, only 1 failed)

suggested_fix: Either: (1) Update the test expectation to accept the trailing slash: `.toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com/')`, or (2) Fix the config reading logic to strip trailing slashes from registry URLs before returning them, whichever matches the intended behavior for registry URL handling in pnpm.
